items.docs: Returns the current version of the running Traefik instance, including the version string, codename, and start date.
Returns an overview of the current Traefik configuration including counts of HTTP, TCP, and UDP routers, services, and middlewares, as well as enabled features and provider information.
Returns the complete raw dynamic configuration data for all routers, services, middlewares, and transports currently loaded from all providers.

Returns an anonymized archive of the running Traefik configuration and runtime metadata. Used by Traefik Labs support to reproduce reported issues without exposing sensitive customer data. The endpoint returns a ZIP archive.
Returns a list of all configured entry points for the Traefik instance, including their names, addresses, and transport configuration.

params.name: name
params.description: The name of the entry point.

items.docs: Returns the configuration of a specific entry point by name.

params.name: search
params.description: Filter results by name using a search string.

params.name: status
params.description: Filter results by status (enabled or disabled).

params.name: per_page
params.description: Number of results per page.

params.name: page
params.description: Page number for paginated results.

Returns HTTP 200 with body "OK" when the Traefik process is alive and ready. Used for liveness probes in container orchestration. Note that the ping endpoint is typically configured on a separate entry point or path outside the /api prefix.
